replan_history.trigger: Requirement changed — knowledge profile scoring was question-frequency-based, violating basic learning science. Scoring from what the user *asked* (not what they *answered*) produces meaningless metrics. Curriculum-first, test-answer-based redesign required before any deployment.
replan_history.new_agent: Lara (curriculum-specialist) — RAG curriculum expert, owns knowledge-base/ artifacts only
replan_history.slug_schema_change: 6 → 8 slugs. Drop: rag_fundamentals (split), langchain (removed). Add: rag_pipeline_architecture, embeddings_and_similarity, context_and_prompting, evaluation_and_metrics. Keep: chunking_strategies, vector_databases, retrieval_methods, production_patterns.

replan_history.trigger: UI quality — NiceGUI default appearance deemed insufficient for portfolio presentation. Team Lead requires a modern, visually striking UI that conveys 'wow' on first impression.
replan_history.new_agent: Aria (frontend) — already active; 4 new UI redesign commits assigned
replan_history.scope_rule: Visual-only changes to src/app/ui.py. Hard rule: no streaming logic, no auth handlers, no state touched in any of the 4 commits.
replan_history.consulted: Aria (design direction + commit structure) + Mira (scope and timing review). Both consulted before replan drafted.

replan_history.trigger: Requirement changed — Team Lead wants a landing page as the first user-facing page (app entry point), plus full UI overhaul of auth and chat pages to match the JSX UI kit in UI_Design/ui_kits/app/. Each page implemented as a separate commit. Landing page routes unauthenticated users away from /login to /landing.
replan_history.new_agent: none — Aria already active; 3 new UI commits assigned
replan_history.scope_rule: Visual-only changes to src/app/ui.py. Hard rule: no streaming logic, no auth handlers, no state touched in any of the 3 commits.
replan_history.routing_decision: Option A — new /landing route + one-line redirect change in index(). No route restructure. Auth pages and chat stay at existing paths.
replan_history.consulted: Aria (routing + CSS isolation + canvas timing + commit scope). Approved after Aria consultation.

replan_history.trigger: Requirement changed — structured module progression (phase-gated advancement), MCQ question format, and new-user onboarding placement added to product scope. Scoped with Mira before replan. Commit 32 ui-chat-shell confirmed done by Team Lead.
replan_history.new_agent: none — Lara, Nova, Aria already active; new commits assigned to their existing domains
replan_history.key_decisions: soft gate (redirect not refuse); advancement questions are MCQ only until open-question scorer validated; onboarding is self-report + 2–3 diagnostic questions, skippable; phase gate math already exists in scoring.py — commit 34 wires it into assess_node._select_test_slug()
replan_history.consulted: Mira (two rounds — progression gating + assessment format). Approved.

replan_history.trigger: Requirement changed + bugs discovered — Mira/Nova learning flow review identified 5 scoring/gate bugs (% 8 question index, passive delta decay, min-session guard, intermediate Phase 1 lockout, silent phase unlock) and 3 new requirements: LangChain topic re-added to curriculum, phase unlock UX with celebration moment, RAG Specialist agent for practitioner-depth content.
replan_history.new_agent: RAG Specialist — practitioner-depth content author; owns knowledge-base/curriculum/questions/ depth; never touches src/ or Lara's structure files
replan_history.parallel_waves_added: Wave F: 39 ∥ 40 (scoring fixes ∥ LangChain curriculum); Wave G: 44 ∥ 45 (phase unlock UI ∥ RAG Specialist content)

replan_history.trigger: Curriculum completeness review + entrepreneurial direction — adaptive engine takes priority over infra. Panel consultation (Mira, Lara, RAG Specialist, Nova) identified: curriculum is 'understand RAG' not 'build RAG from scratch'; document ingestion missing; langchain_fundamentals conflicts with concept-first identity; langgraph_fundamentals gap (app itself uses it); novice questions too hard; question bank too thin for repetition-free sessions. Decision: 7 new commits before deployment.
replan_history.new_agent: none — existing agents cover all new work (Nova, Lara, RAG Specialist)
replan_history.key_decisions: langchain_fundamentals archived not deleted; langgraph_fundamentals is concepts-only (no Python API); AI-generated questions (C52) gated on bank-expansion (C51) completing; mastery_level field already exists in AgentState — no schema changes needed for C46; 4-phase curriculum restructure explicitly deferred to post-ship based on user feedback
replan_history.parallel_waves_added: Wave I: 46 ∥ 47 (src/ vs knowledge-base/); Wave J: 48 ∥ 49 (content vs curriculum-map); Wave K: 51 ∥ 52 (content vs code)
replan_history.consulted: Mira (product), Lara (curriculum), RAG Specialist (content), Nova (technical) — all four consulted before replan drafted.

replan_history.trigger: Requirement changed — Team Lead registered as first-time Novice user and identified 4 UX gaps via Mira + Nova consultation: cold first-time welcome, under-personalized welcome back, weak novice persona tone, inconsistent markdown formatting.
replan_history.new_agent: none — Nova and Aria already active
replan_history.key_decisions: 45.5 and 45.6 are parallel-eligible (different files: rag.py vs ui.py) — Wave H. Both insert before infra phase (46). Nova diagnosed no code bug in user_level routing — root cause is prompt quality only.
replan_history.consulted: Mira (product review) + Nova (technical diagnosis). Both consulted before replan drafted.

replan_history.trigger: Requirement changed — Team Lead wants the Knowledge Profile sidebar redesigned to match the updated two-tab UI kit. New design adds 'Current' tab (active module + topic checklist with gradient checkmarks) and 'Overview' tab (all modules with progress bars and locked-state dimming).
replan_history.scope_rule: Visual-only change to profile_panel() in src/app/ui.py. Hard rule: no other functions touched, no API calls changed, no data structure changed.
replan_history.file_path_correction: Reference files are in UI_Design/app/ not UI_Design/ui_kits/app/ — spec uses correct paths
replan_history.nova_assessment: Nova not needed — tab state is pure NiceGUI Python state (mutable list); data transformation is dict reshaping in the UI layer only
replan_history.gate_wave: Viktor (new Python logic) + Sage (user data rendered) + Mira (explicitly requested by Team Lead)

claude_decisions.decision: Monitoring stack (Prometheus, Grafana, ELK) stays in prod compose
claude_decisions.reason: Team Lead: portfolio must demonstrate production-grade self-evaluation and metrics visibility

claude_decisions.decision: AgentState designed for full arc in Commit 07
claude_decisions.reason: Nova flagged: retroactive state schema changes cascade through compiled LangGraph graph — design once for commits 07-17

claude_decisions.decision: graph.invoke() synchronous inside asyncio.to_thread() — SUPERSEDED by streaming decision 2026-05-09
claude_decisions.reason: Nova: consistent with existing chain.py pattern. Async graph would require rewriting all synchronous node I/O. Superseded: Eran Mani redesigned to graph.astream_events() + SSE StreamingResponse (Commit 10) for production streaming.

claude_decisions.decision: graph.astream_events() + SSE StreamingResponse replaces synchronous graph.invoke()
claude_decisions.reason: Eran Mani: this is a production system — streaming responses are a hard requirement. LangGraph's astream_events() emits on_chat_model_stream events token-by-token; chat.py returns StreamingResponse(media_type='text/event-stream').

claude_decisions.decision: AgentState uses Annotated[list[BaseMessage], add_messages] — not conversation_history: str
claude_decisions.reason: Eran Mani: production streaming requires LangGraph's native message management. add_messages reducer accumulates turns; session_id is passed as thread_id in graph config, enabling MemorySaver checkpointer to reconstruct history automatically.

claude_decisions.decision: SessionMemory class deleted in Commit 10 — LangGraph MemorySaver replaces it
claude_decisions.reason: Eran Mani: LangGraph's checkpointer handles cross-turn persistence natively via thread_id config. The SessionMemory class (src/rag/memory/conversation.py) is redundant and must not survive Commit 10.

claude_decisions.decision: Cache key incorporates user_level from Commit 17 onward
claude_decisions.reason: Nova: query-level cache shared across users at different mastery levels causes silent correctness bugs
